questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: In the context of India's textile and apparel sector, which of the following statements best describes its current global and domestic standing?

options.text: India is the world's largest exporter of textiles, contributing over 10% to global trade.
The sector is the largest contributor to India's GDP, exceeding the share of the services sector.
India possesses the world's second-largest manufacturing capacity in textiles but ranks lower in global export share.
The textile industry accounts for nearly half of India's total industrial production.

questions.explanation: India has the 2nd largest manufacturing capacity globally (after China) but is the 6th largest exporter with a 3.91% share. This indicates a gap between capacity and export competitiveness. It contributes ~2.3% to GDP and 13% to industrial production, making options A, B, and D incorrect.
questions.takeaway: India's textile sector has high manufacturing capacity but faces challenges in translating that into a dominant global export share.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India has the 2nd largest manufacturing capacity (after China) in textiles and is 6th largest exporter with share of 3.91% of the global trade. It contributes ~ 2.3% of GDP, 13% to industrial production.
